attributes.action: Fund GP practice in England adequately to avoid having to charge patients
attributes.additional_details:
GP's in England will be balloted on a 'Plan B' that would allow GP's freedom to provide private services. This includes a proposed 'means tested' approach to appointments whereby patients are charged.
The BMA feels that these drastic measures need to be considered to keep GP practice afloat.

The NHS was meant to be free

The Health Secretary needs to get round the table with the BMA and seriously engage in how to tackle this problem without charging the general population for healthcare.
attributes.background:
GP's will be balloted on a means tested approach to patient appointments. 

The Government needs to get round the table with the BMA and seriously engage in how to tackle this problem without charging the general population for healthcare.

departments.name: Department of Health and Social Care

rejection.details:
You may wish to sign this open petition:
